questions.question_text: In Oklahoma, is a real estate license required to manage property for others?
distractors: No, property management is unregulated
Only for commercial properties
Only if managing 10+ units
questions.explanation: Oklahoma requires a real estate license to manage property for others for compensation. Exemptions exist for owners managing their own property and certain on-site managers.

questions.question_text: A property owner hires a full-time on-site manager for their apartment complex. Does the manager need a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, certain on-site managers are exempt
distractors: Yes, all property managers need a license
Only if the complex has 50+ units
Only if the manager collects rent
questions.explanation: Certain on-site managers are exempt from licensing requirements. The exemption also covers owners managing their own property directly.

questions.question_text: An investor asks a friend to manage their rental properties for a monthly fee. Does the friend need a license?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, managing for compensation requires a license
distractors: No, friends are exempt from licensing
Only if managing more than 3 properties
Not if the fee is under $500 per month
questions.explanation: Managing property for others for compensation requires a real estate license regardless of the relationship between the parties. The friend would need to work under a licensed broker.

questions.question_text: A landlord manages their own 20-unit apartment building. Do they need a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners managing their own property are exempt
distractors: Yes, buildings over 10 units require a license
Yes, all rental property managers need a license
Only if they charge tenants a management fee
questions.explanation: Property owners managing their own property are exempt from real estate licensing requirements, regardless of the number of units they own and manage.

questions.question_text: Which of the following persons must hold a real estate license in Oklahoma?
questions.correct_answer: A company hired to manage rentals for investors
distractors: An owner managing their own rental house
An on-site apartment manager
A tenant collecting rent for a co-tenant
questions.explanation: A company managing property for others for compensation needs a real estate license. Owners managing their own property and certain on-site managers are exempt.

questions.question_text: An Oklahoma property management agreement must be in what form?
distractors: Verbal is acceptable
Either written or verbal
Notarized and recorded
questions.explanation: Property management agreements must be in writing. They must specify the services to be provided, compensation, duration, and trust account handling procedures.

questions.question_text: A property management agreement must specify all of the following EXCEPT:
distractors: Services to be provided
Duration of the agreement
Trust account handling procedures
questions.explanation: PM agreements must specify services, compensation, duration, and trust account handling. Internal commission splits between the broker and their associates are not required in the PM agreement.

questions.question_text: A property manager collects rent and security deposits. Where must these funds be maintained?
distractors: In the property manager's personal account
In the property owner's bank account
In any account chosen by the manager
questions.explanation: Rent and security deposits collected by a property manager must be held in the broker's trust account. The broker must maintain separate trust accounts for tenant deposits and rent.

questions.question_text: An owner asks a property manager to start work immediately under a verbal agreement. What should the manager do?
questions.correct_answer: Obtain a written agreement before starting
distractors: Begin work and formalize later
Start work if the owner emails confirmation
Begin work with a handshake agreement
questions.explanation: Property management agreements must be in writing. The manager should insist on executing a written agreement specifying services, compensation, and trust account procedures before starting work.

questions.question_text: A PM agreement expires but the manager continues collecting rent. What is the risk?
questions.correct_answer: Operating without a valid written agreement
distractors: No risk if the owner is satisfied
The manager becomes the owner's agent
Only a risk if OREC audits the account
questions.explanation: A PM agreement must be in writing and current. Operating without a valid agreement puts the manager at regulatory risk and may not properly define duties and trust account obligations.

questions.question_text: Does the BRA apply to property management brokerage services?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, all BRA duties and disclosures apply
distractors: No, the BRA only covers sales transactions
Only confidentiality duties apply to PM
Only if the PM also handles sales
questions.explanation: The BRA applies to all brokerage services, including property management. The same duties to all parties and to the party served apply when managing rental properties.

questions.question_text: A property manager serves both the landlord and a tenant in a lease transaction. What BRA requirement applies?
questions.correct_answer: Written disclosure to both before signing the lease
distractors: No disclosure needed for lease transactions
Only verbal disclosure to the tenant
Written disclosure to the landlord only
questions.explanation: The BRA's requirement for written disclosure when serving both parties applies to lease transactions just as it does to sales. Both the landlord and tenant must receive written notice.

questions.question_text: Where must tenant security deposits be held when collected by a property manager?
questions.correct_answer: In an escrow account at a federally insured institution
distractors: In the property manager's operating account
In the property owner's personal account
In any account designated by the tenant
questions.explanation: Tenant security deposits must be held in escrow accounts at federally insured institutions in Oklahoma, in compliance with both the Landlord-Tenant Act and trust account requirements.

questions.question_text: A property manager providing brokerage services to a landlord owes which duties to a prospective tenant?
distractors: No duties to prospective tenants
Full fiduciary duties to all parties
Only a duty not to discriminate
questions.explanation: Under the BRA, mandatory duties to ALL parties include honesty, reasonable skill/care, presenting offers, maintaining confidentiality, and compliance with laws. These apply even to the other party.

questions.question_text: A tenant confides their maximum rent budget to the property manager, who is serving the landlord. May the manager share this with the landlord?
distractors: Yes, the manager serves the landlord
Yes, if it helps negotiate the lease
Only with the tenant's verbal permission
questions.explanation: The BRA duty of confidentiality applies to ALL parties, including tenants. The tenant's financial information shared in confidence cannot be disclosed even to the party being served.
